metadata.notes: agent=no — agy has NO user-facing rewind/checkpoint/restore/fork command (palette enumerated live); transcript CHECKPOINT lines are auto-generated, not user-triggered.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

**agent=no.** Checkpoint-rewind requires a user-triggered command/shortcut to restore an earlier checkpoint (claudecode Esc+Esc / /rewind; codex /fork). The `agy` CLI exposes NO such surface. I enumerated the full live slash-command palette (38 commands) under tmux: there is NO `/rewind`, `/checkpoint`, `/restore`, or `/fork`. `agy --help` lists no rewind flag and no rewind keybinding surfaced in /help. The transcript DOES contain auto-generated `SYSTEM`/`CHECKPOINT` lines (observed in every real transcript) — but these are the planner's own automatic checkpoints, NOT a user-triggered rewind, and the parser explicitly SKIPS them (`parser.go:71`, the default case). There is no way for a user (or a driver) to rewind the conversation to an earlier checkpoint from the CLI.

**daemon=n/a.** With no agent surface to trigger a rewind, there is no rewind signal (no transcript truncation, no $rewindTo marker, no fork) for the daemon to observe; the pillar is n/a (no feature → frozen).

**driver=ready** is moot for a frozen cell — the keys/slash step types exist but there is no agent command to navigate.
caveats: Re-assess if a future agy release exposes a user-facing rewind (slash command, keybinding, or the IDE's checkpoint UI surfacing through the CLI store). Antigravity's CHECKPOINT transcript lines suggest the engine maintains checkpoints internally; if a rewind command is later added, the daemon would observe either an in-place truncation or a fork (a new <conv-id>), and this cell flips to a real cell.
The Antigravity IDE (not installed here) may surface a rewind UI, but that is out of scope for the agy CLI; a future IDE-store recording could revisit this.

sources.note: SYSTEM/CHECKPOINT lines are SKIPPED — they are auto-generated planner checkpoints, not user rewinds

recipe.notes: agent_supports=no — agy exposes no user-facing rewind/checkpoint/restore/fork command (live palette enumeration); the transcript's CHECKPOINT lines are auto-generated and parser-skipped. No recipe; this cell is frozen.
